confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_3e624efe-8fd6-4e3a-8154-7b9a34578847 read back as promoted

containment: The architect rejected the wrong self-resolution and reran explicit `--identity architect`.
No runtime binding mutation was performed in Narada proper.
The deferred command for explicit architect binding was reported instead of guessing a runtime handle.
corrective_action: Change operator-surface self-resolution so active roster assignment cannot determine identity by itself.
Require self-resolution to prefer explicit session identity, operator-surface binding identity, or an admitted runtime/session assertion.
If only active roster assignment is available, return self_resolution_ambiguous or self_resolution_untrusted with repair guidance.
Expose requested identity, resolved identity, resolution source, and trust class in bind-focused output.

incident.risk: A role session can be misidentified from active work assignment, causing operator-surface binding, routing, or delivery to attach to the wrong identity.
incident.safe_outcome: runtime_locus_required deferral prevented local runtime binding mutation

preventive_action: Add regression coverage where an Architect thread sees Builder active work and `--as self` must not resolve to Builder.
Add a role-boundary guard for operator-surface mutation commands that refuses cross-role self-resolution based only on projections.
Route this through directed-obligation / role-loop work so assignment state remains a work-selection input, not identity authority.
root_cause_hypothesis: Self identity resolution is using active roster assignment as an authority source for agent identity.
Task/work assignment is being treated as identity evidence instead of a weak contextual projection.
The bind-focused path does not require a stable session identity assertion before resolving --as self.
payload.summary: When narada.architect ran `narada operator-surface bind-focused --as self`, self-resolution returned identity `builder` with source `active_roster_assignment`. The current thread identity was Architect, but roster/task activity was allowed to override role/session identity. The command correctly deferred mutation instead of binding, but the self-resolution result was wrong and could have caused a cross-role binding if a runtime locus and handle had been supplied.
payload.title: CAPA: self-bind resolved Architect session as Builder from active roster assignment
verification: `narada operator-surface bind-focused --as self` in an Architect session never resolves to Builder solely because Builder has an active assignment.
The command either resolves through admitted session identity or defers with an explicit self-resolution repair path.
No runtime binding mutation can proceed when self-resolution source is only active_roster_assignment.
